attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

'../Common/Common';\\nimport GameView from '../GameView';\\n\\nconst { ccclass, property } = _decorator;\\n\\n/**@ch 免費遊戲進場事件 */\\nexport const FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT = {\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 播放進場動畫（In → Loop → 等待 → Out）\\n     * @param (選填) callback 完成後回調\\n     */\\n    PLAY: \\\"FREE_GAME_INTRO_PLAY\\\",\\n}\\n\\n/** 動畫事件名稱（Spine 動畫內嵌的事件標記） */\\nconst AnimEvent = {\\n    /**@ch 進場動畫觸發：隱藏其他 UI */\\n    HideOtherUI: \\\"MG_Off\\\",\\n    /**@ch 退場動畫觸發：顯示其他 UI */\\n    ShowOtherUI: \\\"FG_On\\\",\\n} as const;\\n\\n/** 動畫名稱：H = 橫版, S = 直版 */\\nconst AnimName = {\\n    /**@ch 進場動畫 */\\n    Enter: { H: \\\"In_H\\\", S: \\\"In_S\\\" },\\n    /**@ch 循環動畫 */\\n    Loop: { H: \\\"Loop_H\\\", S: \\\"Loop_S\\\" },\\n    /**@ch 退場動畫 */\\n    Out: { H: \\\"Out_H\\\", S: \\\"Out_S\\\" },\\n} as const;\\n\\n/**@ch 免費遊戲進場演出 */\\n@ccclass('FreeGameIntro')\\nexport class FreeGameIntro extends Component {\\n    //#region Constants\\n    /**@ch 需要註冊的事件列表 */\\n    private static readonly EVENTS_TO_REGISTER = [\\n        FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY,\\n    ];\\n    //#endregion Constants\\n\\n    //#region Properties\\n    @property({\\n        type: InLoopOutSpine,\\n        tooltip: \\\"進場動畫 Spine（控制 In / Loop / Out）\\\"\\n    })\\n    private m_introSpine: InLoopOutSpine = null;\\n\\n    @property({\\n        tooltip: \\\"Loop 循環等待時間（秒），時間到後播放 Out\\\"\\n    })\\n    private m_loopDuration: number = 2;\\n    //#endregion Properties\\n\\n    //#region Variables\\n    /**@ch 是否已註冊事件 */\\n    private m_isEventRegistered: boolean = false;\\n\\n    /**@ch 是否正在播放進場 */\\n    private m_isPlaying: boolean = false;\\n\\n    /**@ch 遊戲視圖 */\\n    private m_gameView: GameView = null;\\n    //#endregion Variables\\n\\n    //#region Lifecycle\\n    onLoad() {\\n        this.RegisterEvent();\\n    }\\n\\n    onDestroy() {\\n        this.UnregisterEvent();\\n        this.m_gameView = null;\\n    }\\n    //#endregion Lifecycle\\n\\n    //#region Event\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 註冊事件\\n     */\\n    private RegisterEvent() {\\n        if (this.m_isEventRegistered) return;\\n        FreeGameIntro.EVENTS_TO_REGISTER.forEach(eventName => {\\n            eventManager.Register(eventName, this);\\n        });\\n        this.m_isEventRegistered = true;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 取消註冊事件\\n     */\\n    private UnregisterEvent() {\\n        if (!this.m_isEventRegistered) return;\\n        FreeGameIntro.EVENTS_TO_REGISTER.forEach(eventName => {\\n            eventManager.UnRegister(eventName, this);\\n        });\\n        this.m_isEventRegistered = false;\\n    }\\n\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 事件處理器\\n     * @param event 事件名稱\\n     * @param args 參數\\n     */\\n    OnEvent(event: string, ...args: any[]) {\\n        switch (event) {\\n            case FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY:\\n                this.PlayIntro(args[0]);\\n                break;\\n        }\\n    }\\n    //#endregion Event\\n\\n    //#region Methods\\n content.thinking:
Now I can see the complete implementation! The user requested that FreeGameIntro.ts should add a property Label to display the total FreeGame rounds, and the event should also include a parameter for the round count.

The changes made:
1. Imported Label from 'cc'
2. Updated FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY documentation to note the totalRound parameter
3. Added m_totalRoundLabel property (type: Label) to display the total FreeGame rounds
4. Updated OnEvent to pass args[0] and args[1] to PlayIntro (totalRound and callback)
5. Updated PlayIntro method signature to accept totalRound: number as first parameter
6. Added logic to set m_totalRoundLabel.string to display the totalRound value

This is a complete feature implementation. I should create an observation now.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Added round count display to FreeGame intro animation</title>
  <subtitle>FreeGameIntro component now accepts and displays total FreeGame rounds during entrance animation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FreeGameIntro.ts now has m_totalRoundLabel Label property for displaying total round count</fact>
    <fact>FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY event signature updated to accept totalRound as first parameter</fact>
    <fact>PlayIntro method signature changed to PlayIntro(totalRound: number, callback?: () => void)</fact>
    <fact>PlayIntro sets m_totalRoundLabel.string to totalRound.toString() when label exists</fact>
    <fact>OnEvent handler passes args[0] (totalRound) and args[1] (callback) to PlayIntro</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>FreeGameIntro component was extended to display the total number of FreeGame rounds earned during the entrance animation. A new @property m_totalRoundLabel of type Label was added to the component, allowing designers to bind a UI label in the editor. The FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY event now requires a totalRound parameter as its first argument, with the callback remaining optional as the second argument. When PlayIntro executes, it checks if m_totalRoundLabel exists and sets its string value to the totalRound count before playing the animation sequence. This allows players to see how many FreeGame rounds they've won during the intro animation. The change maintains backward compatibility with the callback pattern while extending the event to carry round count data.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>assets/Script/GameView.ts</file>
    <file>assets/Script/GameState/EnterFreeState.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/FreeGame/FreeGameIntro.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
Now I see the implementation is complete. EnterFreeState.ts was updated to pass the FreeTotalRound value from RoundInfo when dispatching the FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY event. This completes the data flow:

1. EnterFreeState gets FreeTotalRound from server data (RoundInfo.FreeTotalRound)
2. It dispatches the event with that value as the first parameter
3. FreeGameIntro receives it and displays it in the Label

This is a complete feature implementation. I should create an observation for this change as well.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Connected FreeTotalRound data flow to FreeGame intro</title>
  <subtitle>EnterFreeState now passes server's FreeTotalRound count to intro animation event</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>EnterFreeState.PlayFreeGameEnterAnim updated FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY dispatch call</fact>
    <fact>Event dispatch now includes this.m_gameView.RoundInfo.FreeTotalRound ?? 0 as first parameter</fact>
    <fact>FreeTotalRound value originates from server RoundInfo protobuf message</fact>
    <fact>Data flow complete: server → RoundInfo → EnterFreeState → FreeGameIntro → Label display</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>EnterFreeState was updated to wire up the complete data flow for displaying FreeGame round counts during the intro animation. When dispatching FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY in PlayFreeGameEnterAnim, the state now passes this.m_gameView.RoundInfo.FreeTotalRound (defaulting to 0 if null) as the first argument, followed by the callback. This connects the server-provided round count from the protobuf RoundInfo message to the FreeGameIntro component's display logic. The round count flows from the server response through GameView's RoundInfo property to the event dispatch, where FreeGameIntro receives it and updates the m_totalRoundLabel. This completes the feature implementation for showing players how many FreeGame rounds they've won during the entrance animation.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/GameState/EnterFreeState.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
The user is showing me an edit to GameView.ts where a test hotkey handler (KeyCode.DIGIT_0) is being updated to pass a hardcoded value of 10 as the totalRound parameter when dispatching FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY.

This is updating the test code to match the new event signature that now requires a totalRound parameter. The change is:
- Before: `eventManager.Dispatch( FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY, () => { ... });`
- After: `eventManager.Dispatch( FREE_GAME_INTRO_EVENT.PLAY, 10, () => { ... });`

This is a supporting change to allow the test functionality to work with the updated event signature. I should create an observation for this.
